original_poem:
तुम्हारे हाथ की चूड़ियां  
तुम्हारे हाथ में कैसे खनकती हैं।  
जैसे फूलों से महक उठी हो कोई हवा,  
जैसे सावन में बज उठे हो कहीं घुंघरू।

इन चूड़ियों की खनक में  
तुम्हारी हंसी का एहसास है,  
और ये खनकती हैं यूँ  
जैसे तुम पास ही हो कहीं।
translated_poem:
The bangles on your hand,  
How they tinkle and chime.  
Like the breeze carries the scent of flowers,  
Like anklets jingling in the monsoon time.

In the melody of these bangles,  
I feel the echo of your laughter.  
And they tinkle in such a way,  
As if you're nearby, right after.

preprocessing.alignment_note: Source and translation stanza counts match.

translation_note.value: Bangles as a marker of married or adorned womanhood carries social weight not fully surfaced in translation.
Sawan is the monsoon month associated with longing, Teej, and festivity; 'monsoon time' is approximate.
Ghunghru are ankle bells tied for classical dance or festive wear; 'anklets' loses the performative resonance.
